jokes.text: Futurama is brought to you by Glagnar's Human Rinds. It's a Bunch-a-Munch-a-Crunch-a-Humans!
jokes.setup: Standard TV show sponsorship announcement format
jokes.punchline: Sponsor sells humans as snack food with cheerful advertising jingle
jokes.explanation: Dark, absurdist humor that establishes the show's sci-fi tone through casual cannibalism advertising. Strong economy and originality.

jokes.text: Somebody likes snouts. Is it me?
jokes.setup: Bender observing someone (likely Nibbler) enjoying pig snouts
jokes.punchline: Bender's self-aware confusion about his own preferences
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Bender's robotic confusion about emotions/preferences. Good economy but fairly mild impact.

jokes.text: Oh, my head! My precious head!
jokes.setup: Bender presumably gets hit by something
jokes.punchline: Over-dramatic reaction emphasizing vanity about his head
jokes.explanation: Basic character moment showing Bender's vanity. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Stupid can opener! You killed my father and now you want me!
jokes.setup: Bender dealing with a problematic can opener
jokes.punchline: Escalates to dramatic backstory of can opener murdering his father
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane frustration to absurd revenge drama. Perfect character integration and very quotable.

jokes.text: But why do we keep this time bomb around for that dumb animal?
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Bender is okay after an accident
jokes.punchline: Bender blames the pet (Nibbler) for the dangerous can opener
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's selfishness and tendency to blame others. Good character work but moderate impact.

jokes.text: I'll pet him with both hands!
jokes.setup: Being forced to pet Nibbler to make up after yelling
jokes.punchline: Bender's 'petting' is actually aggressive/violent
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Bender appearing compliant then being violent. Strong character consistency.

jokes.text: My ass! Get off! Get off!
jokes.setup: Nibbler is apparently attacking/biting Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's panicked reaction to being bitten on the butt
jokes.explanation: Basic slapstick moment. Gets a reaction but relies on crude physical comedy without much wit.

jokes.text: Nothing a lawsuit won't cure. Not you.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's okay after being bitten
jokes.punchline: Plans to sue, then clarifies the concern isn't for the pet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender selfishness - concerned about legal compensation, clarifies he doesn't care about the pet. Good economy.

jokes.text: I got a busted ass! I don't see anyone kissing it!
jokes.setup: Everyone is fussing over Nibbler's chipped fang
jokes.punchline: Bender demands equal attention for his injury, using crude phrasing
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's jealousy and need for attention. Character-appropriate crudeness but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: All right, I'm coming.
jokes.setup: Bender's crude demand for attention to his injured butt
jokes.punchline: Leela's resigned agreement to tend to him
jokes.explanation: Leela's exasperated reaction to Bender's demands. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Remember, Rover gets the pill, and Pepper gets the suppository. Next!
jokes.setup: Busy veterinary office scene
jokes.punchline: Casual distinction between pill and suppository for different pets
jokes.explanation: World-building humor showing the mundane reality of veterinary care. Mild chuckle but functional.

jokes.text: What's that? You have to put him down? No. Huh? What? Terrible shame. Shall I do the honors?
jokes.setup: Vet says the fang injury isn't serious
jokes.punchline: Bender mishears/pretends to mishear, eagerly volunteers to euthanize Nibbler
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor showing Bender's eagerness to get rid of Nibbler. Perfect character integration with good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Next time, I'm keeping it.
jokes.setup: Being told 'No' to euthanizing Nibbler
jokes.punchline: Bender's petulant threat to keep the replacement fang (implying future harm)
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's continued malicious intent toward Nibbler. Good character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: The jaguar wasn't helpful, but he did knock one of my teeth out.
jokes.setup: Vet explaining how he got a replacement fang
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions being attacked by a jaguar patient
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of absurd veterinary hazard. Shows the dangerous reality of this vet's work.

jokes.text: I'm woozy from a gazelle kick earlier, but if he's like a tree...
jokes.setup: Examining rings in Nibbler's fang to determine age
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions another animal attack affecting his judgment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the dangerous vet theme. Shows his professionalism despite constant animal attacks.

jokes.text: Good luck. It'd take a genius to count all those rings.
jokes.setup: Vet says counting the rings would take a genius
jokes.punchline: Sets up Fry to easily solve it despite being an idiot
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Fry's unexpected competence. Functional structure but predictable.

jokes.text: He's 5.
jokes.setup: Complex task that would 'take a genius'
jokes.punchline: Fry instantly solves it with simple answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and misdirection. Subverts expectation of Fry's stupidity with simple competence.

jokes.text: I'd be cuddly if someone gave me a cape.
jokes.setup: Everyone admiring how cuddly Nibbler looks in his new cape
jokes.punchline: Bender's jealous demand for equal treatment
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's jealousy and need for attention. Good character work with nice economy.

jokes.text: And the crowd goes wild! What prize do I get? Cash?
jokes.setup: Bender successfully playing pin the tail on the maggot
jokes.punchline: Immediately demands monetary reward for party game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender greed - turning innocent party game into profit opportunity. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: You call that talent?
jokes.setup: Everyone praising Nibbler for holding a spoon
jokes.punchline: Bender dismissively challenges the pet's abilities
jokes.explanation: Sets up Bender's competitive showboating. Good character motivation but mild impact.

jokes.text: And you smell like one too
jokes.setup: Traditional 'Happy Birthday' song ending with 'and you look like one too'
jokes.punchline: Changed to 'smell like one too' for the pet
jokes.explanation: Minor variation on familiar birthday song. Slight chuckle but fairly lazy writing.

jokes.text: Fine. We'll have rack of Nibbler!
jokes.setup: Asked to cook for Nibbler's birthday party
jokes.punchline: Threatens to cook the guest of honor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly fitting Bender's character. Shows his hostility toward Nibbler escalating to culinary threats.

jokes.text: And this time... if someone jumps out of it, put them in after it's cooked.
jokes.setup: Asking Bender to just make a cake
jokes.punchline: References previous incident of someone jumping out of cake, with morbid cooking instruction
jokes.explanation: Nice callback with dark twist. Shows the crew's experience with Bender's chaotic cooking.

jokes.text: So it's a cake you want, is it? I'll make you a cake you'll never forget.
jokes.setup: Being told to make a normal cake for once
jokes.punchline: Bender's ominous promise suggests the cake will be anything but normal
jokes.explanation: Sets up Bender's vengeful cake-making. Good character consistency and ominous tone.

jokes.text: That'll take care of those rats.
jokes.setup: Bender adding something to his cake batter
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions poisoning rats as ingredient choice
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's casual approach to adding toxic ingredients. Character-appropriate dark humor.

jokes.text: Now, to bake a cake so delicious... they'll have no choice but to love and worship me.
jokes.setup: Bender making cake for the party
jokes.punchline: His goal is not just appreciation but literal worship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender megalomania - escalating simple cake-making to divine ambition. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: There! This'll teach those filthy bastards who's lovable.
jokes.setup: Bender finishing his cake creation
jokes.punchline: Calls his friends 'filthy bastards' while trying to make them love him
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - insulting the people he wants to impress. Shows Bender's contradictory nature brilliantly.

jokes.text: Can't you see I'm using the toilet?
jokes.setup: Someone (likely Leela) walks in on Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender using the toilet as a robot, suggesting he doesn't need it but uses it anyway
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual of robot using human toilet. Good character-specific absurdity about Bender's human affectations.

jokes.text: Step one, I lifted the seat. That was the first annoyance. Am I right, men?
jokes.setup: Explaining how he flushed Nibbler down the toilet
jokes.punchline: Complains about having to lift toilet seat, appeals to male solidarity
jokes.explanation: Bender treating pet murder as minor inconvenience, relating it to domestic complaint. Character-appropriate callousness.

jokes.text: Only one way to find out.
jokes.setup: Leela asks what if he flushed Fry down the toilet
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual willingness to flush Fry to test the hypothesis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - treating friend's hypothetical murder as scientific experiment. Excellent economy and character work.

jokes.text: Yeah. One cantaloupe-sized eye? You ain't winning no beauty pageants, lady.
jokes.setup: Claims to feel sorry for Leela
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults her physical appearance instead of showing sympathy
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's inability to show genuine sympathy. Misdirection from sympathy to insult works well.

jokes.text: The toilet.
jokes.setup: Funeral speech about Nibbler going to a place where Bender hopes to go someday
jokes.punchline: The 'blessed place' is revealed to be the toilet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of funeral eulogy tropes. Perfect economy with maximum comedic impact from single word. Excellent Bender character work.

jokes.text: Give it to me straight, doc. Don't sugarcoat it.
jokes.setup: Bender asking doctor for honest assessment
jokes.punchline: Sets up expectation he wants truth about his condition
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for medical comedy. Functional but predictable structure.

jokes.text: Your entire family died when a plane piloted by your fiancée... crashed into your uninsured home. And you have inoperable cancer. Bet you didn't expect that one!
jokes.setup: Bender asking for straight talk from doctor
jokes.punchline: Absurdly over-the-top tragic diagnosis that's completely unrelated to empathy experiment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of tragedy that completely subverts expectation. Professor's casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Just use a potted plant like Fry!
jokes.setup: Professor needs to tinker in someone's head
jokes.punchline: Bender suggests using Fry, comparing his brain to a plant
jokes.explanation: Good character-based insult of Fry's intelligence. Nice economy comparing brain to houseplant.

jokes.text: Yes, if by 'allow' you mean 'force.'
jokes.setup: Asked if empathy chip will 'allow' Bender to feel emotions
jokes.punchline: Professor clarifies it's actually forcing emotions, not allowing them
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing the Professor's scientific precision and slightly sinister nature. Nice economy.

jokes.text: Now I'll simply tune it to Leela's emotional frequency.
jokes.setup: Technical explanation of empathy chip
jokes.punchline: Emotions treated as radio frequencies that can be tuned
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi humor reducing complex emotions to technical problem with simple solution.

jokes.text: My God, I'm overcome with feelings. I'm experiencing a powerful yearning... to cram my gullet with mackerel heads.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to be overcome with new emotions
jokes.punchline: The 'deep feeling' is just wanting to eat fish
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from profound emotion to mundane hunger. Good structure and character work.

jokes.text: That's me, baby.
jokes.setup: Bender expressing desire for mackerel heads
jokes.punchline: Leela casually confirming it's her craving he's feeling
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's casual attitude toward her weird cravings. Nice economy.

jokes.text: I feel I'm not as smart as Leela... but I also feel relieved that I'm cuter than her.
jokes.setup: Bender experiencing Leela's emotions through the chip
jokes.punchline: Feels Leela's self-deprecation about intelligence but vanity about looks
jokes.explanation: Shows Leela's complex self-image through Bender. Good character insight with mild humor.

jokes.text: Thanks for covering.
jokes.setup: Bender expressing that he feels dumber than Leela
jokes.punchline: Fry thanks him for taking over the 'dumb character' role temporarily
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about character roles in the show. Good self-awareness and economy.

jokes.text: Bingo!
jokes.setup: Bender expressing complex mix of emotions including being nosy and opinionated
jokes.punchline: Professor's excited confirmation that the chip is working perfectly
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction showing Professor's satisfaction with his experiment. Good economy but mild impact.

jokes.text: Happy. I like this feeling. Don't revert to your usual mopey self.
jokes.setup: Leela saying she's happy that Bender can feel her pain
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately criticizes her personality while feeling her emotions
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Bender insulting Leela while literally feeling her emotions. Shows his personality override.

jokes.text: I'm not mopey. You shut up!
jokes.setup: Being called mopey by Bender
jokes.punchline: Leela's defensive reaction proves his point about her moodiness
jokes.explanation: Classic self-defeating defense. Leela's angry reaction confirms Bender's criticism.

jokes.text: Anger, huh? How dare you make me feel anger... you one-eyed jerk with a dead pet?
jokes.setup: Bender experiencing Leela's anger through the chip
jokes.punchline: Gets angry about feeling angry, then insults her with cruel pet reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bender's confusion with emotions. The cruel pet jab maintains his character.

jokes.text: Nibbler loved to eat puppies.
jokes.setup: Fry suggesting getting a puppy to cheer Leela up
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that her pet ate puppies
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny revelation about Nibbler's diet. Good misdirection from cute pet suggestion to predatory reality.

jokes.text: Hell, no! It's Leela's feelings! Why can't she just drink herself happy like a normal person?
jokes.setup: Asked if he misses Nibbler
jokes.punchline: Clarifies the feelings aren't his, suggests alcoholism as normal coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - recommending alcoholism as healthy emotional processing. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: You only get all the guys because you dress like a tramp!
jokes.setup: Feeling jealous of an attractive woman at the bar
jokes.punchline: Bender expressing Leela's female jealousy through his robotic perspective
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast of robot expressing female jealousy. Good character work showing the chip's effects.

jokes.text: I'm so lonely. I'm gonna go eat a bucket of ice cream.
jokes.setup: Left alone while others go on dates
jokes.punchline: Robot expressing classic female emotional eating behavior
jokes.explanation: Good contrast of robotic character expressing stereotypical human female coping behavior.

jokes.text: The spoon's in the foot powder!
jokes.setup: Bender going to eat ice cream
jokes.punchline: Reveals his bizarre kitchen organization
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail about Bender's living situation. Good character-specific weirdness with nice economy.

jokes.text: My friend's cousin's caseworker saw one. It's a widely believed fact.
jokes.setup: Talking about alligators living in sewers
jokes.punchline: Fry's chain of unreliable sources presented as definitive proof
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - multiple degrees of separation treated as solid evidence. Good character work.

jokes.text: Hang on, Nibbler! Uncle Bender's coming to save you!
jokes.setup: Bender deciding to go into the sewers
jokes.punchline: Sudden transformation from pet-hater to heroic 'Uncle Bender'
jokes.explanation: Shows dramatic personality change due to empathy chip. Good character development moment.

jokes.text: What did those humans design this for anyway?
jokes.setup: Bender trying to fit through toilet opening
jokes.punchline: Questions human toilet design while trying to flush himself down it
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about the absurdity of his situation. Decent character work.

jokes.text: Bender, one. Toilet, zero.
jokes.setup: Bender finding a way to get through the toilet
jokes.punchline: Treats plumbing victory like a sports score
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's competitive nature even with inanimate objects. Nice economy.

jokes.text: Have you seen my sombrero?
jokes.setup: Serious moment of Bender potentially being lost
jokes.punchline: Professor's completely unrelated concern about missing hat
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment showing his scattered priorities. Nice contrast with the serious situation.

jokes.text: He flushed himself down the toilet? Who's next?
jokes.setup: Learning Bender flushed himself down toilet
jokes.punchline: Fry treating it as trend rather than emergency
jokes.explanation: Good Fry logic treating bizarre crisis as social trend with casual attitude.

jokes.text: It's your fault. Your emotions made him feel bad. You're right. I feel terrible. Now you're making him feel worse!
jokes.setup: Blaming Leela for Bender's emotional state
jokes.punchline: Creating feedback loop where guilt about causing emotions causes more emotions
jokes.explanation: Excellent recursive logic creating infinite loop of emotional responsibility and guilt.

jokes.text: Mutants are just a ridiculous urban myth.
jokes.setup: Others warning about mutants in the sewers
jokes.punchline: Leela's dismissal of mutants as myth (setting up their immediate appearance)
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for immediate contradiction. Functional but predictable structure.

jokes.text: God bless America.
jokes.setup: List of American toxic waste causing mutations
jokes.punchline: Fry's patriotic response to environmental disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry patriotism despite obvious environmental problems. Good character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: We're under Park Avenue. Ritzy!
jokes.setup: Reading location from guidebook while in sewers
jokes.punchline: Fry's excited reaction to being under expensive neighborhood while in literal sewer
jokes.explanation: Good irony of being impressed by location while in terrible circumstances. Fits Fry's character.

jokes.text: Just think. All this was probably once a charity luncheon for the Met.
jokes.setup: Looking at sewer waste
jokes.punchline: Imagining fancy cultural event as source of current filth
jokes.explanation: Creative connection between high culture and sewage. Good absurdist observation.

jokes.text: Mutants!
jokes.setup: Just declared mutants were a myth
jokes.punchline: Immediate shocked reaction to seeing actual mutants
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure. Predictable but well-executed immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: I have three arms!
jokes.setup: Being told they're harmless
jokes.punchline: Protests by mentioning physical difference, mishears 'harmless' as 'armless'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay misunderstanding. Sets up the harmless/armless distinction joke.

jokes.text: I said, harmless not armless! Lay off him. He only has one ear.
jokes.setup: Confusion about harmless vs armless
jokes.punchline: Clarifies wordplay then defends another mutant's disability
jokes.explanation: Completes the wordplay and adds another disability joke. Good structure and pacing.

jokes.text: Shouldn't you eat my brain? You're mutants!
jokes.setup: Meeting friendly mutants
jokes.punchline: Fry's disappointment that they don't match horror movie stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment showing his pop culture expectations. Character-appropriate disappointment.

jokes.text: Mutants? Perhaps you are the mutants.
jokes.setup: Being called mutants
jokes.punchline: Philosophical reversal suggesting surface dwellers are the real mutants
jokes.explanation: Good philosophical humor about perspective. Sets up the mutant leader as thoughtful.

jokes.text: Dwayne, have you looked in a mirror lately?
jokes.setup: Leader's philosophical comment about who are the real mutants
jokes.punchline: Another mutant's practical reminder about their obvious mutations
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of philosophical moment with practical reality. Nice character dynamic.

jokes.text: Perhaps your civilization... is the sewer of an even greater society above you.
jokes.setup: Being told they live in a sewer
jokes.punchline: Suggests infinite hierarchy of civilizations above each other
jokes.explanation: Clever philosophical escalation with recursive logic. Good quotable concept.

jokes.text: No, we're on top. Daylight and everything.
jokes.setup: Complex philosophical suggestion about hierarchical civilizations
jokes.punchline: Fry's simple, literal response missing the deeper point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry response - completely literal interpretation of philosophical concept. Great character work.

jokes.text: Just crumpled porn and Ayn Rand.
jokes.setup: Showing off their library made from flushed materials
jokes.punchline: Library contents reveal what gets flushed down toilets
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary equating pornography and Ayn Rand as equally disposable. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Over here is our church.
jokes.setup: Showing religious facility in sewer community
jokes.punchline: Sets up reveal of what mutants worship
jokes.explanation: Basic setup line for upcoming visual punchline about their religious object.

jokes.text: You guys worship an unexploded nuclear bomb?
jokes.setup: Being shown the mutants' church
jokes.punchline: Church object is revealed to be dangerous nuclear weapon
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about mutant society. Shows their dangerous improvisation.

jokes.text: Nobody's that observant. It's mainly for Christmas and Easter.
jokes.setup: Surprised observation about nuclear bomb worship
jokes.punchline: Casual response treating dangerous worship as casual holiday tradition
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan treatment of nuclear worship as casual religious practice. Great economy.

jokes.text: That's an urban legend.
jokes.setup: Asked about alligators flushed down toilets
jokes.punchline: Mutant dismisses urban legend while being a living urban legend himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - mythical creature dismissing other myths. Excellent callback structure with great economy.

jokes.text: They're our pets. When they grow too large, we flush them into the sub-sewer.
jokes.setup: Explaining the crocodiles
jokes.punchline: Reveals infinite regression of flushing pets to lower levels
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation creating infinite hierarchy of sewer levels. Original concept with good structure.

jokes.text: There's a race of sub-mutants there.
jokes.setup: Mentioning sub-sewer where crocodiles go
jokes.punchline: Even more mutated beings exist at lower level
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the infinite sewer hierarchy concept. Nice economy.

jokes.text: Please! That's just a sub-urban legend.
jokes.setup: Talk of sub-mutants in sub-sewer
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'suburban' and 'sub-urban' while referencing urban legend theme
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining the sub-sewer concept with urban legend theme. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: Then I suppose you also don't believe in El Chupakneebray.
jokes.setup: Fry's skepticism about sub-sewer creatures
jokes.punchline: Parody name combining El Chupacabra with kneecap reference
jokes.explanation: Good monster name parody combining cryptid legend with anatomy reference for humor.

jokes.text: He creeps in the midnight hush / Silent as a low-flow toilet flush / But sooner or later / He'll eat you whole / And half your alligator
jokes.setup: Legend of El Chupakneebray
jokes.punchline: Monster song with toilet humor and 'crocodile' correction
jokes.explanation: Creative monster legend with toilet-themed lyrics. Good world-building humor with decent quotability.

jokes.text: Crocodile.
jokes.setup: Song lyrics saying 'alligator'
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction during dramatic monster song
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect economy. Deflates dramatic moment with pedantry.

jokes.text: Nice try, Leela... but we've all seen Zapp Brannigan's web page.
jokes.setup: Leela volunteers as virgin sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals everyone knows about her sexual history via Zapp's website
jokes.explanation: Excellent burn with perfect character integration. Combines Bender's cruelty with modern internet implications. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, I made myself feel bad.
jokes.setup: Just delivered cruel burn to Leela
jokes.punchline: Empathy chip makes him feel bad about his own insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing the empathy chip's effects. Bender immediately regretting his own cruelty is excellent character development.

jokes.text: And rip her shirt a little.
jokes.setup: Setting up virgin sacrifice scene
jokes.punchline: Adds gratuitous sexualization to ritual preparation
jokes.explanation: Relies on cheap sexualization humor. Gets a reaction but lacks wit or cleverness.

jokes.text: Nightfall? How can you tell down here?
jokes.setup: Told that nightfall is coming
jokes.punchline: Logical question about time perception in windowless sewer
jokes.explanation: Good practical question showing Fry's occasional logical thinking. Nice economy.

jokes.text: The tide is coming in.
jokes.setup: Asked how they can tell time underground
jokes.punchline: Reveals sewers have tidal patterns like ocean
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about sewer ecology. Creates vivid, disgusting image with good economy.

jokes.text: I'm too scared! Your fear is being transmitted to Bender!
jokes.setup: Bender unable to fight monster due to fear
jokes.punchline: Reveals the empathy chip is transmitting others' fear to Bender
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing unintended consequences of empathy chip. Creates comedy from character paralysis.

jokes.text: The secret is not giving a rat's ass about anyone... and only caring about things that you want... that you deserve, that the world owes you!
jokes.setup: Teaching Leela how not to care
jokes.punchline: Accidentally gives perfect description of narcissistic selfishness as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from caring advice to narcissism manifesto. Very quotable and psychologically sharp.

jokes.text: Bender is back! I'll save you, Nibbler!
jokes.setup: Leela successfully stops caring
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately returns to heroic form
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing the empathy chip solution working. Nice character moment.

jokes.text: Know what else I could use? A weekend at a fancy spa. And a Toblerone.
jokes.setup: Monster fight happening while Leela learns selfishness
jokes.punchline: Continues listing material wants during crisis, ends with specific candy
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of selfish desires. The specific Toblerone adds nice character detail.

jokes.text: It'll be ages before another guitar string is flushed.
jokes.setup: Trying to create legend song about Bender
jokes.punchline: Can't make music because they depend on flushed instruments
jokes.explanation: Good detail about the reality of scavenged society. Shows practical limitations of sewer living.

jokes.text: You won't believe this, but the empathy chip burned out.
jokes.setup: Bender demanding chip removal
jokes.punchline: Chip already stopped working on its own
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the twist about Bender's emotions. Creates false hope about character growth.

jokes.text: The emotion you felt for Nibbler was your own!
jokes.setup: Revealing the chip burned out
jokes.punchline: Suggests Bender's rescue was genuine emotion, not artificial
jokes.explanation: Good character development fake-out. Sets up hope for Bender's emotional growth.

jokes.text: No, I'm wrong. The chip was running at triple capacity. And I still barely felt anything. Good night, losers!
jokes.setup: Hope that Bender learned emotional lesson
jokes.punchline: Reveals chip was working extra hard and he still felt almost nothing, dismisses friends
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expected character growth. Perfect structure building hope then crushing it. Excellent Bender characterization maintaining his emotional emptiness.

jokes.text: Maybe Bender didn't learn from me... but I learned something from him.
jokes.setup: Bender revealing he learned nothing
jokes.punchline: Leela claims she learned something instead
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom lesson reversal. Attempts to salvage meaning from Bender's lack of growth.

jokes.text: So long, jerkwads!
jokes.setup: Leela trying to find meaning in the experience
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual dismissal of everyone as 'jerkwads'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ending - maintains his character by insulting everyone. Good callback to his consistent rudeness.
